qa_pairs.question: When will the Vulcan cluster be available?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster will be available on April 15, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is the login node for Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The login node for Vulcan is vulcan.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the Globus collection for Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Globus collection for Vulcan is accessible via the link: https://app.globus.org/file-manager?origin_id=97bda3da-a723-4dc0-ba7e-728f35183b43, named 'Vulcan Globus v5'.

qa_pairs.question: What is the URL for Vulcan's System Status Page?
qa_pairs.answer: The URL for Vulcan's System Status Page is https://status.alliancecan.ca/system/Vulcan.

qa_pairs.question: What is the web portal for Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The portal for Vulcan is https://portal.vulcan.alliancecan.ca.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is a cluster dedicated to meeting the needs of the Canadian scientific Artificial Intelligence community.

qa_pairs.question: Where is the Vulcan cluster located?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster is located at the University of Alberta.

qa_pairs.question: Who manages the Vulcan cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster is managed by the University of Alberta and Amii.

qa_pairs.question: What inspired the name 'Vulcan' for the cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: The cluster is named after the town Vulcan, AB, located in southern Alberta.

qa_pairs.question: Is Vulcan part of the Pan-Canadian AI Compute Environment?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the Vulcan cluster is part of the Pan-Canadian AI Compute Environment (PAICE).

qa_pairs.question: Is internet access generally available from Vulcan's compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: No, internet access is not generally available from the compute nodes on Vulcan.

qa_pairs.question: How can users access the internet from Vulcan's compute nodes?
qa_pairs.answer: A globally available Squid proxy is enabled by default with certain domains whitelisted. Users should contact technical support if they need to access a domain not on the whitelist.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum job duration allowed on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The maximum duration for jobs on Vulcan is 7 days.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to use Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan is currently open to all researchers conducting AI research or applying AI methods in their research.

qa_pairs.question: How can I get access to log in to Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: To log in to Vulcan, you must request access in CCDB by visiting https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/me/access_services.

qa_pairs.question: What is required to submit jobs on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: To submit jobs on Vulcan, you must be a member of an AIP RAP.

qa_pairs.question: What should a Principal Investigator (PI) do if they don't have an AIP RAP to submit jobs?
qa_pairs.answer: If you are a PI and don't already have an AIP RAP, you should apply for General Access to PAICE Systems via https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca/paice/general_access_to_paice_systems.

qa_pairs.question: How can a PI add other researchers to their AIP RAP on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can add other researchers by going to the 'Resource Allocation Projects' table on the CCDB home page (https://ccdb.alliancecan.ca), locating their AIP project (with the 'aip-' prefix), clicking on it, then clicking 'Manage RAP memberships' at the bottom of the page, and entering the user's CCRI in the 'Add Members' section.

qa_pairs.question: How many nodes does the Vulcan cluster have?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster has 252 nodes.

qa_pairs.question: What model are the compute nodes on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The compute nodes on Vulcan are Dell R760xa models.

qa_pairs.question: What type of CPU is used in each Vulcan node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node uses 2 x Intel Xeon Gold 6448Y CPUs.

qa_pairs.question: How many cores does each Vulcan node have?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 64 cores.

qa_pairs.question: How much system memory does each Vulcan node possess?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 512 GB of system memory.

qa_pairs.question: What GPUs are installed in each Vulcan node and what is their memory?
qa_pairs.answer: Each Vulcan node has 4 x NVIDIA L40s GPUs, each with 48GB of memory.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total number of GPUs available on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster has a total of 1008 GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: What type of storage system does Vulcan utilize?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan's storage system uses a combination of NVMe flash and HDD storage.

qa_pairs.question: What platform does Vulcan's storage system run on?
qa_pairs.answer: Vulcan's storage system runs on the Dell PowerScale platform.

qa_pairs.question: What is the total usable capacity of Vulcan's storage system?
qa_pairs.answer: The storage system on Vulcan has a total usable capacity of approximately 5PB.

qa_pairs.question: Which storage spaces are hosted on the Dell PowerScale system?
qa_pairs.answer: The Home, Scratch, and Project storage spaces are all on the same Dell PowerScale system.

qa_pairs.question: What are the characteristics of the Home space on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Home space is where /home directories are located, has a small fixed quota, is not allocated via RAS or RAC, and has daily backup.

qa_pairs.question: What are the characteristics of the Scratch space on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Scratch space is for active or temporary storage, is not allocated, has a large fixed quota per user, and inactive data will be purged.

qa_pairs.question: What are the characteristics of the Project space on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: The Project space has a large adjustable quota per project and includes daily backup.

qa_pairs.question: What is the network interconnect speed between nodes on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: Nodes on Vulcan are interconnected with 100Gbps Ethernet.

qa_pairs.question: What network technology is enabled for interconnects on Vulcan?
qa_pairs.answer: RoCE (RDMA over Converged Ethernet) is enabled for network interconnects on Vulcan.

qa_pairs.question: What scheduler does the Vulcan cluster use to manage user workloads?
qa_pairs.answer: The Vulcan cluster uses the Slurm scheduler to run user workloads.
